text.title: Architecture Decision Records Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Architecture Decision Records. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every ADR contains five sections: Context, Decision (active voice 'We will...'), Alternatives Considered (≥2 with pros/cons + rejection reason), Consequences (positive/negative/neutral), Related Decisions.
rule.rationale: Missing Alternatives is the most common quality failure; without it, debates recur.

rule.statement: New ADRs are created with status 'Proposed'; only a human flips to 'Accepted'.
rule.rationale: Self-approval skips review where affected teams identify conflicts.

rule.statement: Accepted ADRs are not edited; supersede by creating a new ADR and updating the old one's status to 'Superseded by ADR-NNN'.
rule.rationale: Editing Accepted ADRs destroys the historical record needed to understand why earlier choices made sense.

rule.statement: ADRs live in docs/adr/NNN-title.md with sequential numbering from 001; numbers are never reused even if an ADR is deleted.
rule.rationale: Stable citation scheme lets design.md reference AD-X without later renumbering.

rule.statement: Each ADR stays under 400 words core text; longer means the decision scope is too broad and must be split.
rule.rationale: Length over 400 words typically means implementation details that belong in design.md.

rule.statement: Alternatives, consequences, and rejection reasons are grounded in spec.md / design.md inputs; agents must not fabricate.
rule.rationale: Fabricated alternatives undermine the credibility of the entire ADR.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-architecture-decision-records.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
